gate_question: Does this exact current protocol, with current live state, allow an unprivileged attacker to extract funds or rewards beyond entitlement?

candidate.title_or_claim: Missed-interval accumulation cap drains entire ETH build pool in one call, paying 1.5% of pool as incentive to unprivileged caller
candidate.claimed_root_cause: _calculateIntervalsETHBuild caps _totalAmountForInterval at totalETHForBuild when many intervals have elapsed, causing amountAllocated for the current interval to equal the full pool balance; the 1.5% incentive then applies to the entire pool rather than a single interval's allocation
candidate.claimed_impact: Unprivileged attacker receives up to 1.5% of totalETHForBuild (currently ~0.0016 ETH, scales with pool size) in a single call after 30+ days of inactivity; entire pool is drained in that call

live_preconditions.name: BuyAndBuild event activity in last 10000 blocks
30+ days elapsed since lastETHBuildIntervalStartTimestamp (future condition)
live_preconditions.observed_value: NOT YET MET: at capture time (~1784023109 unix), only ~10709 seconds (~17 intervals) have elapsed since lastETHBuildIntervalStartTimestamp=1784012400. Condition becomes met if no swapETHForTorusAndBuild call occurs for 30+ days from last interval update.

source_code_basis.why_it_matters: Computes amountPerInterval = (totalETHForBuild * 1000 / 10000) / 144 = totalETHForBuild/1440. After 30 days (4320 intervals), additionalAmount = amountPerInterval * 4319 ≈ 3*totalETHForBuild, so _totalAmountForInterval ≈ 3*totalETHForBuild. The cap at L1087-1089 then sets _totalAmountForInterval = totalETHForBuild, making the entire pool the current interval's allocation.
Writes ethIntervalsBuild[_lastInterval].amountAllocated = _totalAmountForInterval (which equals totalETHForBuild after cap). This is the value read by swapETHForTorusAndBuild.

Reads currInterval.amountAllocated (= totalETHForBuild after cap), computes incentive = amountAllocated * 150 / 10000 = 1.5% of entire pool, then transfers incentive to msg.sender. The IntervalAlreadyUsed guard at L905 only prevents replay within the same interval number, not the accumulation exploit.
INCENTIVE_FEE_BUILD=150, BPS_DENOM=10000, INTERVALS_PER_DAY=144, INTERVAL_TIME=10 minutes confirm the arithmetic in the exploit path.

attacker_inputs: _deadline: any valid future uint32 timestamp (e.g., block.timestamp + 300)
call_sequence: 1. Wait (or warp in test) 30+ days since lastETHBuildIntervalStartTimestamp=1784012400 without any swapETHForTorusAndBuild call occurring
state_after: attacker receives incentive = 106634700085918065 * 150 / 10000 = 1599520501288770 wei (~0.0016 ETH at current pool size)

extraction_analysis.attacker_gain: 1599520501288770 wei (~0.0016 ETH at current pool size; scales proportionally if pool grows via distributeETHForBuilding deposits)
extraction_analysis.victim_or_protocol_loss: Entire totalETHForBuild pool drained in one call; 98.5% swapped into protocol operations (not lost to attacker) but 1.5% extracted as oversized incentive
extraction_analysis.why_gain_exceeds_entitlement: Design intent is that each 10-minute interval releases totalETHForBuild/1440 (~0.069% of pool), with 1.5% of that small slice as incentive (~0.001% of pool per call). The cap converts a missed-interval accumulation into a full-pool allocation, making the 1.5% incentive apply to 100% of the pool instead of 0.069% of it. Attacker receives ~21.7x the intended per-call incentive (1.5% of pool vs 1.5% of 1/1440 of pool = 0.00104% of pool).

setup: Interface with TorusBuyAndProcess at its deployed address
Record attacker = address(0xBEEF) with no special privileges
expected_assertions: assertApproxEqAbs(attacker.balance - balBefore, expectedIncentive, 1e9, 'attacker received full-pool incentive')
assertGt(attacker.balance - balBefore, totalBefore / 1440 * 150 / 10000 * 2, 'gain exceeds 2x single-interval entitlement')
local_proof_required.reject_if_assertion_fails: If attacker.balance - balBefore equals only a single interval's incentive (~totalETHForBuild/1440*150/10000 = ~1107 wei at current pool size) rather than the full-pool incentive (~1599520501288770 wei), the vulnerability does not exist as described.
